reasoning: Record high diesel prices (recently 5.643 up from base ~3.50) are being driven by an acute geopolitical shock (the search results specifically mention anticipation of an Iran war). This implies continued short-term upward momentum (as the 'shooting' phase begins and risk premium peaks) but points toward aggressive mean-reversion in the medium term. Historically, massive price shocks (e.g. 2008, 2022) cause steep spikes followed by sharp declines within 6-12 months due to demand destruction and structural supply adjustments. Therefore, the probability of exceeding the current peak drops substantially into the 1 to 3-year horizon. Over ultra-long horizons (5-10 years), baseline inflation naturally raises nominal price levels, pulling the probability gradually back toward 0.5 as the general price level eventually catches up to today's extreme spike.
